title: The NOW page: activity ledger + roadmap as a derived view — intent lives in the graph as human-vouched proposed decisions
context: Owner wanted a 'hot file' ledgering recent work + a roadmap. A hand-maintained NOW.md is exactly the rotting artifact the wiki exists to kill — and 'what's next' had no first-class home in the graph.
decision: Roadmap items are PROPOSED decisions: recording intent is a capture; shipping it (accept/supersede) removes it from the roadmap with zero file maintenance. The hot view is <wiki>/now.md — a reserved generated page (manifest id '_now') in the freshness closure: last N decisions by date (any status — supersession IS activity) on top, live proposed decisions below, hashed over the item snapshot, wiki-stale drift + --heal like every artifact, home-scoped reads (private home unions the overlay). Roadmap lists ONLY human-vouched proposals (provenance includes human_confirmed); auto-synthesized drafts — also status 'proposed' but describing work already done — are counted with a pointer to `hunch review`, never listed, so real plans aren't buried. No topic pins on the page by design: a ledger entry's decision going stale is history, not drift; the input hash alone re-renders. `hunch now` prints the same data in the terminal (--private for the union, labeled do-not-paste).
consequences: Recording any decision stales now.md (by design — it IS the activity feed); heal after capture sessions
The roadmap travels via git and renders in both homes; private roadmap items stay overlay-only like everything else
hunch review becomes the pressure valve the now-view points at when drafts pile up
alternatives_rejected: A hand-maintained NOW.md/ROADMAP.md: rots by Thursday; the whole week was about killing that artifact class
Roadmap from all status='proposed' records: auto-drafted proposals flooded it with 9 already-shipped items on first dogfood — vouched-intent-only is the semantic
Topic pins on ledger entries: every later supersession would fire doc-anchor-stale on a HISTORY page — noise with no heal meaning
Commits in the ledger: would couple wikiStatus to git state and stale the page on every commit; decision-level activity is the right grain
